label: PI-099 authored + resolved in one close-out — fix apply_close_out.py section ordering so conversation POSTs before session. Three close-outs in 24 hours hit the complete_session_requires_conversation rule on first apply because session was being POSTed before its required inbound edge existed. Single-line swap of _SECTIONS[0] and _SECTIONS[1] resolves it. THIS close-out's own apply is the meta-validation: if PI-099 works, SES-107 lands in one pass with no retry — unlike the three preceding close-outs (SES-101, SES-103, SES-105) that all needed a second attempt.

session.session_title: PI-099 — fix apply_close_out section ordering; meta-validation via this session's own one-pass apply
session.session_description:
Single Claude Code session. Doug's prompt after the SES-105 PI-018 close-out: 'lets file the apply_close_out ordering issue as a PI then knock it out.' Both halves done in this session: PI-099 authored AND resolved.

---

Topics covered:

Seed prompt: "yes" (continuing from the prior turn's offer to file and fix the apply_close_out ordering issue).

1. Surveyed apply_close_out.py to locate _SECTIONS (lines ~241-251). Confirmed current order: session, conversation, work_tickets, planning_items, commits, decisions, references, resolves_planning_items, addresses_planning_items. The session entry runs FIRST.

2. Traced the failure mode: post-PI-073 / DEC-314 introduced the complete_session_requires_conversation validation rule — a session in complete status requires at least one inbound conversation_belongs_to_session edge at create-validation time. Apply_close_out POSTs the session before the conversation, so the inbound edge doesn't exist yet, and the session creation 422s.

3. Reviewed context-population (lines 580-592): context['session_identifier'] and context['conversation_identifier'] are read from payload data BEFORE the section loop, not from POST responses. So shape helpers that read those (commits' _shape_commit; resolves' _shape_resolves_pi; addresses' _shape_addresses_pi) work regardless of section order.

4. Reviewed the existing _inline_membership_edges hoisting (lines ~605-612): a prior fix hoists session_belongs_to_workstream into the session block's inline references and conversation_belongs_to_session into the conversation block's inline references so the access layer sees the membership edge at create-validation time. The hoisting handles outbound edges; the current bug is on the inbound edge side (session needs to see the inbound edge from the conversation, but the conversation hasn't been POSTed yet).

5. Dispatched a single sub-agent with the technical scope (swap _SECTIONS[0] and _SECTIONS[1]; update the docstring's order diagram; add tests covering single-pass apply). Brief explicitly noted the empirical evidence — three first-apply failures in 24 hours — and the safe-because-X reasoning (conversation creation tolerates a not-yet-existent target).

6. Sub-agent returned commit 9f04266. Verified the conversation access layer at conversations.py:115 has the explicit comment 'there is no complete-requires-session-edge rule' — the membership IS the session linkage and runs from create, but the target session row is NOT required to exist for the conversation POST. Confirms the swap is safe.

7. Agent also surfaced four other inbound-edge requirements in the broader access layer (close_out_payloads.py:105 payload_requires_producing_conversation_edge; close_out_payloads.py:150 applied_payload_requires_successful_deposit_event_edge; work_tickets.py:102 consumed_work_ticket_requires_consumption_edge; deposit_events.py:213 deposit_event_requires_applies_close_out_payload_edge). None are tripping current close-outs because they're handled by lazy-create paths or atomic POSTs at apply time. Noted for a future apply-pipeline-validator PI (likely a PI-090 expansion or sibling) but out of scope for PI-099.

8. Tests added in test_apply_close_out.py: TestPI099SectionOrdering with two tests — section-order assertion and a single-pass-apply test that authors a minimal payload (new-shape session_identifier/session_title/session_description/session_medium fields) and verifies the session lands in complete status without needing a retry. Both pass. Pre-existing 15 failures in the file remain (all PI-073-schema-mismatch — payloads use legacy identifier/title/topics_covered shape) but were verified non-regressing.

9. Authoring this close-out as the meta-validation. The expectation: SES-107's own apply succeeds in ONE pass (no DEP retry pattern) because PI-099's fix is now active. The actual deposit_event sequence will be DEP-NNN single instead of DEP-NNN-failed plus DEP-(NNN+1)-success.

---

In flight at end:

- The four other inbound-edge requirements surfaced by the agent (close_out_payloads payload_requires_producing_conversation_edge; close_out_payloads applied_payload_requires_successful_deposit_event_edge; work_tickets consumed_work_ticket_requires_consumption_edge; deposit_events deposit_event_requires_applies_close_out_payload_edge) — none breaking today, structurally identical pattern. PI-090 (apply-pipeline validator) would catch ordering violations at pre-flight; a sibling PI could fix any future occurrences by either making the rules tolerant-of-deferred-edge-presence or by atomic-batch POSTing the linked records.
- Sibling parallel session SES-106 landed during my agent's work (resolved PI-096 / PI-097 / PI-098 — the executive_summary backfill review pass). My SES-107 reservation re-keyed from SES-106 to SES-107, CNV-008 to CNV-009; PI-099 head unaffected.
session.session_executive_summary: Fixed the recurring partial-apply pattern that has cost three close-outs a manual retry each over the last 24 hours. Apply_close_out.py was POSTing the session before the conversation that emits the session's required inbound conversation_belongs_to_session edge. One-line swap of _SECTIONS[0] and _SECTIONS[1] restores the correct order. This close-out's own one-pass apply (no DEP retry) is the meta-validation: if PI-099 worked, SES-107 lands cleanly the first time — unlike SES-101, SES-103, and SES-105 which all needed two passes. The agent also surfaced four other structurally-identical inbound-edge ordering risks in the access layer that aren't tripping today but warrant a future apply-pipeline-validator PI.

session_medium_metadata.meta_validation: this close-out's own apply is the proof — if PI-099 works, SES-107 lands in one pass

Fix is a one-line _SECTIONS swap. Meta-validate by having this very close-out apply succeed in one pass.

The conversation also surfaced four other structurally-similar inbound-edge ordering risks in the access layer that don't trip today but warrant future attention.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-099 — fix apply_close_out section ordering so conversation POSTs before session
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-099 — fix apply_close_out section ordering so conversation POSTs before session

Every close-out's first apply attempt has been failing on the new
post-PI-073 ``complete_session_requires_conversation`` validation rule
since the redesign landed. Three back-to-back occurrences (SES-101
first apply at DEP-088, SES-103 first apply at DEP-091, SES-105 first
apply at DEP-094) traced to the same cause: ``_SECTIONS`` orders
``session`` before ``conversation``, but ``complete`` sessions require
at least one inbound ``conversation_belongs_to_session`` edge at
create-validation time. The conversation that emits that edge wasn't
POSTed yet.

The fix is a one-line list swap: process ``conversation`` before
``session``. Empirically proven safe — conversation creation already
tolerates a not-yet-existent ``conversation_belongs_to_session``
target (the three first-apply failures above ALL successfully created
the conversation; the session was the only thing that failed). With
the swap, the conversation's inline membership edge to the session is
in place before session validation runs, and the apply completes in
one pass.

Changes:

- ``crmbuilder-v2/scripts/apply_close_out.py`` — swap the first two
  entries of ``_SECTIONS``. Update the module docstring's apply-order
  diagram to match.
- ``tests/crmbuilder_v2/scripts/test_apply_close_out.py`` — assert
  the new section order, plus a single-pass-apply test that authors a
  minimal close-out payload and verifies the session lands in
  ``complete`` status without needing a retry.

The fix is independent of and complementary to PI-090 (apply-pipeline
validator). PI-090 will catch broader payload-shape violations at
pre-flight; PI-099 fixes the specific ordering bug that was tripping
every close-out.

From PI-099.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

planning_items.title: Fix apply_close_out.py section ordering — conversation must POST before session to satisfy complete_session_requires_conversation validation
planning_items.description:
Every close-out's first apply attempt has been failing on the post-PI-073 / DEC-314 ``complete_session_requires_conversation`` validation rule, requiring a manual retry to converge. Three back-to-back occurrences in 24 hours (SES-101 first apply at DEP-088; SES-103 first apply at DEP-091; SES-105 first apply at DEP-094) — each had to be re-run, each successfully created the session on second attempt. The cause: ``_SECTIONS`` in ``crmbuilder-v2/scripts/apply_close_out.py`` orders ``session`` before ``conversation``, but a ``complete`` session requires at least one inbound ``conversation_belongs_to_session`` edge at create-validation time. The conversation that emits that edge is processed in the NEXT section and hasn't been POSTed yet when the session validation fires.

Fix: swap ``_SECTIONS[0]`` and ``_SECTIONS[1]`` so ``conversation`` is processed before ``session``. Update the module docstring's apply-order diagram. Add tests covering single-pass apply (no retry needed).

Safe because: conversation creation already tolerates a not-yet-existent ``conversation_belongs_to_session`` target — the three first-apply failures above ALL successfully created the conversation row plus its inline reference edge; only the session failed. The access layer (``conversations.py``) has an explicit comment at the edge-validation logic: 'there is no complete-requires-session-edge rule' — the membership IS the session linkage and runs from create, not deferred to complete status. Context dictionary (``apply_close_out.py:580-592``) is pre-populated from payload data before the section loop, so no shape-helper depends on POST-response ordering.

Independent of PI-090 (apply-pipeline validator), which will catch broader payload-shape violations at pre-flight; PI-099 fixes the specific ordering bug. Related but distinct: four other inbound-edge requirements in the access layer (``close_out_payloads.py:105`` payload_requires_producing_conversation_edge; ``close_out_payloads.py:150`` applied_payload_requires_successful_deposit_event_edge; ``work_tickets.py:102`` consumed_work_ticket_requires_consumption_edge; ``deposit_events.py:213`` deposit_event_requires_applies_close_out_payload_edge) are structurally identical but currently handled by lazy-create paths or atomic POSTs at apply time — not tripping today. Worth a follow-on PI if any of those start tripping in future workflows.

Meta-validation: this PI is filed AND resolved in the same close-out (SES-107 / CNV-009). The close-out's own apply is the proof — if PI-099 worked, the apply succeeds in one pass; if not, it would fail with the same complete_session_requires_conversation error.
